title: A highly-sensitive graphene-based immunoassay procedure for the detection of human lipocalin-2
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"hulyr","text":"We report a highly-sensitive graphene nanoplatelets (GNPs)-based sandwich enzyme-linked immunosorbent immunoassay (ELISA) procedure for the detection of human lipocalin-2 (LCN2). It involves the functionalization of 96-well microtiter plate (MTP) with GNPs (dispersed in 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (APTES)). APTES generates the free amino groups on MTP and GNPs, which are used for the  leach-proof covalent binding of anti-LCN2 capture antibody by 1-ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl) carbodiimide hydrochloride (EDC) based heterobifunctional crosslinking. The developed immunoassay detects LCN2 in the range of 0.6-5120 pg mL-1 with limit of detection and analytical sensitivity of 0.7 and 1 pg mL-1, respectively. It is the most sensitive immunoassay for LCN2, which has 80-fold higher analytical sensitivity and 3-fold reduced immunoassay duration than the commercial sandwich ELISA kit. The anti-LCN2 antibody-bound MTPs, stored at 4°C in 0.1M phosphate buffered saline (PBS), were highly stable as they did not show any decrease in functional activity for 8 weeks.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":626,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":698,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dlykc","text":"The highly sensitive detection of LCN21, also known as neutrophil gelatinase-associated lipocalin (NGAL), is desired in clinical diagnosis as it is an important prognostic and diagnostic biomarker for several medical conditions. It is a small 25 kDa glycoprotein, which is found in neutrophils and certain epithelia such as renal tubules. The LCN2 level in healthy humans is 0.7 ng mL-1, which increases in cases of ischemic2 or nephrotoxic injury3, cardiovascular surgery, kidney transplants4-6, coronary heart disease7-8, fatty liver disease, obesity9, bacterial infections10-11 and cancer12-15. The down-regulation of LCN2 occurs in glioblastoma16, ovarian cancer14, microglial cells17 and in response to excessive exposure to high electromagnetic fields18. \r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":38,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":384,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":424,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":447,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":492,"length":3},{"style":"sup","offset":519,"length":3},{"style":"sup","offset":552,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":575,"length":5},{"style":"sup","offset":591,"length":5},{"style":"sup","offset":648,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":666,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":686,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":757,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"p2adk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gtg5y","text":"\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pimrj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1a4u3","text":"The developed GNPs-based sandwich ELISA procedure19 has higher sensitivity and reduced immunoassay duration in comparison to commercial sandwich ELISA kits and our previously developed immunoassay procedures20-24. It involves the APTES functionalization of GNPs and MTP, and EDC-based heterobifunctional crosslinking of anti-LCN2 capture antibodies (Fig. 1). The high sensitivity of the developed procedure is mainly due to the increased surface area provided by GNPs, which leads to higher immobilization density of anti-LCN2 capture antibodies. The developed immunoassay detects the entire pathophysiological range of LCN2 in the real sample matrices i.e. diluted human serum, plasma and whole blood. It has a very high precision as it correlates well with the conventional ELISA. Therefore, it is ideal for mass-use clinical and industrial settings as the anti-LCN2 antibody-bound MTPs, stored at 4°C in 0.1 M phosphate buffered saline (PBS), did not lose their functional activity even after 8 weeks. Moreover, it is a generic sandwich ELISA procedure that can also be employed in in vitro diagnostics (IVD) for the detection of other disease biomarkers and analytes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":350,"length":6},{"style":"italic","offset":1085,"length":8},{"style":"sup","offset":49,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":207,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

The buffers were made with ultrapure water (UPW). It can be stored indefinitely.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27},{"style":"sub","offset":1,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":4,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":61,"length":1},{"style":"sub","offset":64,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Put 100 μL of 1% (w/v) KOH into the wells of 96-well MTP followed by incubation for 5 min at 37°C and subsequent washing with 300 μL of UPW five times. Washing can also be performed with an automatic plate washer. CRITICAL STEP KOH treatment is optimal at 5 min but it should not be longer than 10 min, which can cause surface aberrations that impact the bioanalytical performance of developed immunoassay.

Incubate the KOH-treated MTP with 100 μL of GNPs (1 mg mL -1 ) in 0.25% (v/v) APTES for 1 h at RT inside the fume cabinet. Wash the GNPs-functionalized wells extensively with UPW (as described in step 1). CRITICAL STEP One mg of GNPs should be mixed with 1 mL of 0.25% APTES and dispersed by keeping in an ultrasonic bath for 1 h before use.

Incubate 990 μL of anti-LCN2 capture antibody (2 µg mL -1 ) with 10 μL of EDC (4 mg mL -1 ) for 15 min at 37°C. CRITICAL STEP The optimized concentration of EDC is critical for the crosslinking of antibodies.
Put 100 µL of EDC-activated anti-LCN2 antibody to the GNPs-functionalized wells followed by incubation for 1 h at 37°C and subsequent washing with 400 µL of PBST five times.

Block the antibody-bound wells with 400 µL of 1% (w/v) BSA for 30 min at 37°C followed by extensive PBST washing (as mentioned in step 4). CRITICAL STEP Use diagnostic grade BSA that does not interfere with the immunoassay components. Also filter the prepared BSA in order to remove any contaminating substances.
Add 100 µL of varying LCN2 concentrations (0.6-5120 pg mL -1 ) to different BSA-blocked wells. Incubate for 1 h at 37°C and wash extensively with PBST (as stated in step 4).
Add 100 µL of biotinylated anti-LCN2 antibody in each of the LCN2-captured wells. Incubate for 1 h at 37°C and wash extensively with PBS (as mentioned in step 4).
Add 100 µL of SA-HRP conjugate to the biotinylated anti-LCN2 antibody treated wells. Incubate for 20 min at 37°C and wash extensively with PBST (as mentioned in step 4).
Add 100 µL of TMB-H 2 O 2 mixture to the wells of MTP and incubate at RT for 20 min to develop color.
Stop the enzyme-substrate reaction by adding 50 µL of 2 N H 2 SO 4 to each well.

Measure the absorbance at a primary wavelength of 450 nm and secondary wavelength of 540 nm in the Tecan MTP reader. CRITICAL STEP Determine the absorbance after 5 min of stopping the enzyme substrate reaction so that the reaction is effectively stopped. But the reading should be taken before 10 min.
Troubleshooting advice is provided in Table 1 .
Steps 1-2, Surface activation and GNPs-functionalization: 1 h 5 min Steps 3-5, Antibody immobilization and BSA blocking: 1 h 30 min Steps 6-11, LCN2 sandwich ELISA: 2 h 40 min

The developed GNPs-based sandwich ELISA 19 detected 0.6-5120 pg mL -1 of LCN2 ( Fig. 2A ) with linearity in the range of 80-2560 pg mL -1 . The limit of detection (LOD) and analytical sensitivity of LCN2 immunoassay were 0.7 pg mL -1 and 1 pg mL -1 , respectively. Various experimental controls were employed that demonstrated the absence of non-specific interactions between the immunoassay components ( Fig. 2B ). The inter- and intra-day variability were in the range of 1-10.2 % and 2-13.7 %, respectively, while the half-effective concentration (EC50) was 435 pg mL -1 . The intraday variability was determined by six assay repeats in a day, while the interday variability was calculated from six assay repeats on six different days. The developed immunoassay has high precision as it correlates well with the commercial sandwich ELISA. It enables LCN2 detection in atual sample matrices ( Fig. 2C ), as demonstrated by the analysis of human LCN2-spiked samples that were prepared by mixing various human LCN2 concentrations in 1:1000 diluted human plasma, serum and whole blood. The developed procedure significantly decreased the overall assay duration by more than 3-fold i.e. from 20 h (commercial sandwich ELISA) to 5.5 h. It has 80-fold better analytical sensitivity (1 pg mL -1 ) than that of commercial LCN2 ELISA (80 pg mL -1 ). Moreover, it has better bioanalytical performance (lower LOD and better analytical sensitivity) than our previously developed LCN2 immunoassay 20 . All these immunoassays were performed under identical conditions using the same components to obviate process variability. The higher sensitivity of the developed ELISA is mainly due to the increased surface area provided by GNPs functionalization, which leads to high antibody immobilization density. The antibody immobilization strategy further enables the leach-proof covalent binding of capture anti-LCN2 antibodies by EDC-based crosslinking of its carboxyl groups to the free amino groups on silanized GNPs-functionalized MTP. The anti-LCN2 capture antibody-bound MTPs can be stored at 4°C in 0.1 M PBS for up to 8 weeks without any significant loss of their functional activity ( Fig. 2D ), which is desired for clinical and industrial analysis. The developed procedure is multisubstrate-compatible 19 and can be used to perform sandwich ELISA on various substrates using our previously developed modified MTP format 25 . The multisubstrate-compatibility was due to the generic nature of silanization and GNPs functionalization steps. The developed procedure has got tremendous potential to improve the bioanalytical performance of sandwich ELISA-based IVD kits.
The authors declare no competing financial interests.
Graphene-based immunoassay for human lipocalin-2 by Sandeep Kumar Vashist published in Analytical Biochemistry on 30 Dec, 2013 https://dx.doi.org/10.1016/j.ab.2013.10.022

Figure 1. Schematic of the developed procedure Schematic of the developed graphene nanoplatelets (GNPs)-based sandwich immunoassay procedure for the detection of human lipocalin-2 (LCN2). Figure 2. GNPs-based human LCN2 immunoassay (A) Developed GNPs-based sandwich ELISA to detect human LCN2 and its comparison with our previously developed and conventional sandwich ELISAs. (B) Specific detection of human LCN2 by the developed ELISA using various experimental process controls. (Cap - Capture, Ab - Antibody) (C) Detection of human LCN2 in various sample matrices. (D) Stability of the anti-LCN2 capture antibody-bound MTPs stored in 0.1 M PBS, pH 7.4 at 4°C. All experiments were done in triplicate with the error bars representing the standard deviation.
